title: podman does not forward ipv6 ports
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
podman does not forward ipv6 requests to the container, only ipv4.
Trying to make nginx available via ipv6 fails. Connecting from the host using [::1]:PORT fails, wheras connecting to the nginx server via [::1]:PORT from inside the container works. Also, connecting from the host using 127.0.0.1:PORT works. Connecting to the container vial ipv6 should work or it should be clearly documented that `--publish` only works for connections via ipv4.
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. make a fresh fedora container with nginx installed

2. run container via `podman run --name nginx-static -t -a STDOUT -a STDERR -a STDIN --rm -p 8080:80/tcp  localhost/fednginximage bash`

3. inside the container run `/usr/sbin/nginx`

4. from the host, try to connect to the nginx server via both, `[::1]:8080` and `127.0.0.1:8080`

**Describe the results you received:**
Connecting from the host only works for `127.0.0.1:8080`, but fails for `[::1]:8080`

**Describe the results you expected:**
Connecting from the host should work for both cases.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.10
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  MemFree: 6668619776

    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc9+dev
      commit: 0840802d505e28d3adcbc7a275cbeadbdf64ddec
```

comments.body:
Could you try running slirp4netns with `-6` option?

```console
$ mv slirp4netns slirp4netns.orig
$ cat > slirp4netns << EOF
#!/bin/sh
exec slirp4netns.orig -6 $@
EOF
$ chmod +x slirp4netns
```
(or simply recompile podman with modified source)
A workaround for this issue is to run `socat` on the host
@AkihiroSuda @giuseppe Should we be running with slirp4netns -6 by default?

It seems libslirp doesn't support forwarding IPv6 ports
https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/slirp/libslirp/blob/ad36c04a3ef961a2dbb6dc78dd5322318a7f10b6/src/slirp.c#L1001

Somebody should submit patches to libslirp upstream for supporting IPv6.

Or maybe podman can use RootlessKit's nsenter-based port forwarder which is simpler and faster.
(Used by Rootless Docker)

RootlessKit's port forwarder is untested with IPv6 either, but it should not be too difficult to support IPv6 if it doesn't work with IPv6.
https://github.com/rootless-containers/rootlesskit/tree/master/pkg/port/builtin

> Could you try running slirp4netns with `-6` option?
> 
> ```
> $ mv slirp4netns slirp4netns.orig
> $ cat > slirp4netns << EOF
> #!/bin/sh
> exec slirp4netns.orig -6 $@
> EOF
> $ chmod +x slirp4netns
> ```
> 
> (or simply recompile podman with modified source)

Thanks, I tried, but it did not help. Same result as before.

I tried the same with creating and running the container as root. That does not help either. So maybe remove the `rootless` label?

Also thanks for pointing at `socat`, I will use it as a workaround for now.

> I tried the same with creating and running the container as root. That does not help either. So maybe remove the rootless label?

The result is same but the cause seems different. Could you open a separate issue for discussing rootful IPv6?
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
This is something we want, although no one is working on it at this time.
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
Have we determined if Docker forwards IPV6 ports?  I think we have determined that it did not, so nothing to fix here.   Am I correct @mccv1r0?

That's my understanding as well.
